title: Lesson Quiz: Global Markets and Interdependence
description: Assessment of the concept that Economic connections between countries.

choices.text: The growing connection of countries through trade and economic activity
choices.feedback: Correct. Globalization refers to increasing international economic connection.

choices.text: A system in which government controls all production
choices.feedback: That describes a command economy, not globalization.

choices.text: The decision to stop all imports
choices.feedback: Globalization increases connection rather than ending international exchange.

choices.text: A local market that serves one town only
choices.feedback: This does not describe global economic connection.

questions.prompt: Interdependence means countries can rely on one another for goods, services, or production.

choices.feedback: Correct. Interdependence means countries are economically connected and rely on one another.
This statement is true because interdependence means countries rely on one another.

questions.prompt: Which example best shows a global market?

choices.text: A business sells only to people in one neighborhood
choices.feedback: A global market involves economic exchange across countries.

choices.text: A company buys materials from several countries and sells products worldwide
choices.feedback: Correct. This shows production and exchange taking place across countries.

choices.text: A family shops at one local store
choices.feedback: This does not show international economic connection.

choices.text: A school cafeteria chooses next week's menu
choices.feedback: This is not an example of a global market.

questions.prompt: Why can a disruption in one country affect other countries in a global market?

choices.text: Because countries are often economically interdependent
choices.feedback: Correct. Interdependence means countries and businesses can be affected by changes in other places.

choices.text: Because trade only happens inside one country
choices.feedback: Trade in global markets happens across countries, not only inside one country.

choices.text: Because globalization prevents all shortages and delays
choices.feedback: Globalization does not prevent disruptions. It can spread their effects.

choices.text: Because countries never depend on foreign production
choices.feedback: Countries often do depend on foreign production and trade.

questions.prompt: Global markets connect buyers and sellers across countries.

choices.feedback: Correct. Global markets link economic activity across national borders.
This statement is true because global markets connect countries through trade and exchange.
